title: Dream-Song
content:
Sunlight, moonlight,
         Twilight, starlight - 
     Gloaming at the close of day,
         And an owl calling,
         Cool dews falling
     In a wood of oak and may.

         Lantern-light, taper-light,
         Torchlight, no-light:
     Darkness at the shut of day,
         And lions roaring,
         Their wrath pouring
     In wild waste places far away.

         Elf-light, bat-light,
         Touchwood-light and toad-light,
     And the sea a shimmering gloom of grey,
         And a small face smiling
         In a dream's beguiling
     In a world of wonders far away.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Walter De La Mare, titled "Dream-Song", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
